Committee.CommitteeName: Special Interim Committee on Property Tax Reform
Special Committee on Property Tax Reform

RepresentativeInfo.CapitolAddress: MO House of Representatives, 201 West Capitol Avenue, Room 407-B, Jefferson City MO 65101
RepresentativeInfo.Biography:
State Representative Jim Murphy, a Republican, represents parts of St. Louis County (District 94) in the Missouri House of Representatives. He was elected to his first two-year term in November 2018.
In addition to his legislative duties, Murphy is the owner and operator of Shoppers Rule, Inc. Before starting his own company, he was the Vice President of Sales and Marketing for Tacony Corporation in Fenton.
Murphy graduated from Newport News High School before attending North Carolina State University.
Born in New York, Murphy now resides in St. Louis with his wife, Maryellen. They have three children and 12 grandchildren. Murphy and his wife attend Saint Margaret Mary Alacoque Church.

SponsoredBill.LongTitle: Modifies provisions governing income exempt from earnings tax
Establishes the "Media Literacy and Critical Thinking Act"
Modifies and establishes provisions regarding motor vehicles offenses
Abolishes the death penalty and specifies that any person sentenced to death must be sentenced to life imprisonment without parole
Modifies provisions relating to tax levies by political subdivisions
Modifies provisions relating to employment and creates new requirements relating to the verification of the immigration status of certain persons
Establishes the "Safe Place for Newborns Fund" for the installation of newborn safety incubators
Creates provisions relating to the practice of certain licensed professions
Modifies provisions for ballot summary language adopted by the general assembly
Repeals provisions relating to rebate coupons from manufacturers of intoxicating liquor
Establishes provisions relating to insurance for certain uses of motor vehicles
Requires a county central committee treasurer to accept a candidate's filing fee if the candidate has been affiliated with the party for at least twenty-six weeks prior to the election
Modifies provisions governing liens on property for unpaid bills
Establishes provisions relating to summer camps

Grants use of the House Chamber to Washington Middle School, Oakville Middle School, Bernard Middle School, and Margaret Buerkle Middle School on April 7, 2025, from 9:00 a.m. to 1:00 p.m. for presentation of the Glory Awards

CoSponsoredBill.LongTitle: Modifies provisions relating to income tax
